# Architectural Drawing Description

This is a historical architectural blueprint rendered in brown ink on aged, cream-colored paper, depicting detailed technical drawings of a large Victorian-era building complex. The main composition shows a longitudinal section view at the bottom center, labeled "Longitudinal Section," displaying the building's full side profile with multiple stories, peaked roofs with decorative finials, various window and door openings, and internal structural framework including beams and supports. A tall spire or tower structure rises prominently from the left side of the main building. Two additional architectural detail drawings appear in the upper portion: on the upper right is labeled "Transverse Section" showing a cross-sectional view of the building's interior, and on the upper left is a smaller sectional detail labeled "R. of Gable Gable." The drawings include numerous handwritten dimension notations and measurements in small script throughout. At the bottom of the main section are labeled areas including "Main Part," "House Porter," "Dressmaking," "Notions," and "Millinery." The right edge of the document features a color reference scale bar. The overall document shows age-related wear with visible creasing and staining on the aged paper surface.

520__a-1: This image shows an aged architectural blueprint for the second floor of a building, created by F. McHous & Son, Architects, located at 530 Pine Street. The document is printed on aged, tan-colored paper with visible stains, discoloration, and wear marks throughout. At the top of the page is a color reference scale containing squares of blue, cyan, green, yellow, magenta, pink, white, and dark gray, along with a measurement ruler. The main blueprint features detailed floor plans drawn in brown lines showing multiple rooms, hallways, and spaces with labeled dimensions. The layout includes several "Bed Rooms" (marked with numbers 3-4), bathrooms, living areas, and a central staircase. Rooms are annotated with measurements like "11'6" x 14'" and "13'x 14'6"". The scale notation at the bottom left reads "SCALED EIGHT FEET TO ONE INCH." The title "SECOND FLOOR" appears prominently at the bottom center of the blueprint. The document shows typical architectural drawing conventions with dotted lines indicating walls and construction details, and small circular symbols representing architectural elements like doors and fixtures.

520__a-1:
This is an architectural floor plan drawn on aged paper with visible stains and wear marks, showing the first floor layout of a residential building. At the top of the page is a measurement scale with colored reference bars (navy, cyan, green, yellow, magenta, white, and dark gray). The plan is rendered in brown ink lines on a cream-colored background with yellowing and water staining throughout.

The layout displays a symmetrical design featuring multiple rooms arranged around central spaces. Upper sections contain a "Sitting Room" (11'x14'), kitchen (13'x15"), and various smaller rooms and closets. The middle section shows two prominent rooms labeled "Old Ladies' Dining Room" (20'x30') on the left and "Children's Dining Room" (20'x30') on the right, separated by a central hallway. Below these are additional spaces including "Hall" sections, bedrooms, and other service areas. Room dimensions are marked throughout in feet and inches.

The lower section contains multiple "Bed Rooms," a "Servants' Room," "Sitting Room," and other utility spaces, with a "Vestibule" prominently marked in the center. Staircases are indicated by diagonal hatching in several locations.

At the bottom left, text reads "Scale: Eight Feet to One Inch." The bottom right corner contains "B. M. Dougall & Son, Archts., 330 Pine St. S.F." and the center bottom indicates "First Floor."
